macro_summary: AI infrastructure spending remains the dominant market theme as semiconductor earnings season kicks off. TSMC reports pre-market tomorrow (July 16), serving as the first hard datapoint on AI chip demand this cycle. A cluster of mega-cap catalysts — TSMC (July 16), MSFT (July 29), AAPL (July 30), and FOMC (July 29) — compresses into a two-week window, making defined-risk structures preferred over outright directional bets. Memory markets are signaling structural tightness with SK Hynix's CEO flagging shortages lasting beyond 2030, while NVIDIA trades at a historically cheap valuation despite 85%+ revenue growth.
screening_note: 22 independent overnight screening runs analyzed. Minimum qualifying threshold: 2 appearances. All 5 qualifying tickers cleared this bar; the top 2 appeared in 21-22 of 22 runs, indicating extremely strong consensus.
key_events: TSMC Q2 2026 earnings: July 16 pre-market (~2:00 AM ET) — imminent sector catalyst
MSFT Q4 FY2026 earnings: July 29, 2026 after market close
AAPL Q3 FY2026 earnings: July 30, 2026 after market close
NVDA Q2 FY2027 earnings: expected August 26, 2026
SKHY post-IPO price discovery continues; Barclays PT $330 initiated July 14

ideas.name: SK Hynix Inc. (Nasdaq ADR)
ideas.thesis: SKHY appeared in all 22 overnight screens — the strongest consensus signal of the session. The freshly listed Nasdaq ADR (IPO July 10 at $149, now ~$185-194) is the most direct US-listed pure-play on the AI HBM memory supercycle, with its CEO stating chip shortages could persist beyond 2030 and HBM supply already sold out through 2027. Post-IPO shakeout from $194 to $152 and subsequent rebound has created swing-trade levels with strong institutional sponsorship (second-largest US share sale ever), while ADR-to-Seoul arbitrage continues to drive price discovery volatility.
ideas.catalyst: Barclays Overweight initiation July 14 with $330 PT; Q2 earnings expected late July/early August; TSMC print July 16 as near-term sector read
ideas.edge: IPO priced at $149; Barclays PT $330 implies ~78% upside from ~$185; net income +108% YoY, EPS +104% YoY, ROIC 73.54%; revenue +40.8% YoY; $198 Fibonacci target in near-term sight
ideas.bull_case: Structural AI memory shortage beyond 2030 with HBM supply sold out through 2027 creates durable pricing power; analyst PT of $330 and massive institutional sponsorship support a sustained re-rating as the ADR matures and price discovery settles.
ideas.bear_case: New listing with thin options liquidity and volatile ADR-Seoul arbitrage mechanics; post-IPO lockup dynamics could trigger sharp selling pressure, and a TSMC miss tomorrow could drag the entire memory complex lower.

ideas.name: Microsoft Corporation
ideas.thesis: MSFT appeared in 21 of 22 overnight screens with 15 high-conviction votes, making it the highest-conviction idea in this digest. The stock has been dragged ~20-25% off 52-week highs by IBM's legacy-software warning — a clear valuation dislocation given 40% Azure growth, 23% EPS growth, and AI ARR at $37B growing 123% YoY. With a confirmed earnings date of July 29, the stock trades at its cheapest forward multiple since 2023 against a $589-592 consensus analyst target, creating a structural setup for a re-rating.
ideas.catalyst: Q4 FY2026 earnings July 29, 2026 after market close — 14 days away; FY2027 AI capex guidance within that print is a sector-wide catalyst
ideas.edge: 97 analyst consensus PT $589-592 vs. ~$387 current = >50% gap; trading at ~21-22x NTM earnings, cheapest forward multiple since 2023; Azure guided 39-40% CC growth already in the model
ideas.bull_case: Azure acceleration and $37B AI ARR growing at 123% YoY make the IBM comparison fundamentally flawed; a July 29 beat-and-raise on cloud and AI could catalyze a sharp multiple re-rating toward the $589 consensus target.
ideas.bear_case: If FY2027 capex guidance disappoints or Azure growth decelerates, the stock lacks a near-term fundamental floor given it has already de-rated significantly; earnings binary risk on July 29 is elevated given back-to-back FOMC same day.

ideas.name: NVIDIA Corporation
ideas.thesis: NVDA appeared in 19 of 22 screens with 15 high-conviction votes and sits at a statistically anomalous valuation — forward P/E of 21.7x versus its 5-year average of 72x (Goldman Sachs) — despite 85% revenue growth, 75% gross margins, and HBM supply sold out through most of 2027. A cascade of near-term catalysts (TSMC July 16, MSFT capex guidance July 29, own earnings August 26) provides multiple windows for re-rating, while the mid-summer pullback appears sentiment- and geopolitically-driven rather than fundamental.
ideas.catalyst: TSMC Q2 earnings July 16 (direct GPU shipment read); MSFT FY2027 capex guidance ~July 29; NVDA Q2 FY2027 earnings August 26, 2026
ideas.edge: Forward P/E 21.7x vs. 5-year avg 72x; PEG 0.6 on 85% revenue growth; consensus 12-month PT $301.62 vs. ~$211 current = ~43% upside gap; semi industry Q2 earnings growth forecast 131% per FactSet
ideas.bull_case: A TSMC beat tomorrow validating AI chip demand, combined with MSFT raising capex guidance and hyperscaler AI spend topping $1T, creates a powerful multi-catalyst re-rating sequence into NVDA's own August earnings.
ideas.bear_case: FOMC July 29 binary, geopolitical headline risk (Taiwan strait, Iran), and a 19-of-22 screen consensus that may already be well-owned; a TSMC miss tomorrow could trigger a sharp sector-wide sell-the-news move.

ideas.name: Apple Inc.
ideas.thesis: AAPL appeared in 19 of 22 overnight screens with mixed conviction (10 high, 9 medium), reflecting a split between strong fundamental momentum and valuation richness. The stock has pulled back ~9% from recent highs to ~$284, creating a gap versus the $315 consensus target, with a confirmed July 30 earnings catalyst arriving the day after MSFT reports and FOMC decides — a compressed catalyst cluster that could drive outsized sector momentum or a volatility spike. Eight consecutive EPS beats, a $100B active buyback, and Services revenue at $31B/qtr growing 16% YoY provide a durable floor.
ideas.catalyst: Q3 FY2026 earnings July 30, 2026 after market close — the day after MSFT earnings and FOMC decision; foldable iPhone launch odds at 92% pre-2027
ideas.edge: 8 consecutive EPS beats; $100B buyback active; consensus PT ~$315 vs. ~$284 current (~11% upside); Services at $31B/qtr +16% YoY; $30B Broadcom chip deal extended to 2031 deepens AI integration
ideas.bull_case: A July 29 MSFT beat creates positive sector sentiment heading into AAPL's July 30 print; Services at an all-time high and a deepening AI hardware roadmap (Broadcom deal, foldable iPhone) support a re-rating toward the $315 consensus target.
ideas.bear_case: AAPL trades at ~37x earnings versus NVDA at ~30x despite growing roughly 5x slower, limiting re-rating upside; the compressed MSFT/FOMC/AAPL catalyst cluster on July 29-30 creates asymmetric volatility risk that could overwhelm even a solid beat.

ideas.name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.
ideas.thesis: TSM appeared in 13 of 22 screens with unanimous high-conviction votes (13 high, 0 medium) — the cleanest conviction signal in the digest. With Q2 2026 earnings releasing pre-market tomorrow (July 16, ~2:00 AM ET), TSM is the single most direct read on AI chip demand as the primary logic chip fabricator for NVDA, AAPL, and AMD. An 8-quarter earnings beat streak, sub-16x forward P/E versus fabless peers, and guided 32-33% YoY revenue growth make this a high-quality beat-and-raise candidate that could re-rate the entire semiconductor complex.
ideas.catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings July 16, 2026 pre-market at ~2:00 AM ET — approximately 18 hours away
ideas.edge: Guided Q2 revenue $39.0-40.2B (~33% YoY growth); 8 consecutive EPS beats averaging 8.34% upside; forward P/E ~16x vs. sector; ASML beat signals strong demand chain; global semi sales +104% YoY in May per SIA
ideas.bull_case: A beat-and-raise on AI chip revenue with raised full-year guidance above the >30% baseline directly validates NVDA's demand pipeline and acts as a rising tide for SKHY, NVDA, and the broader AI semiconductor trade.
ideas.bear_case: Binary event risk with very limited remaining time to position; any guidance caution on CoWoS packaging capacity, Taiwan geopolitical tension, or softer smartphone demand could trigger a sharp sector-wide reversal given how much AI optimism is priced in.
